title: Nyaya Sutra
edition_note: Pilot passages and grader notes only. Verify against the edition and commentary tradition you choose before public release.

passages.translation_or_note: Liberation is attained through true knowledge of the Nyaya categories, including pramana, prameya, doubt, purpose, example, doctrine, syllogistic members, reasoning, ascertainment, debate forms, fallacies, quibbles, rejoinders, and points of defeat.
passages.grader_use: Use for broad Nyaya category questions and for locating hetvabhasa within the Nyaya project.

passages.translation_or_note: Perception, inference, comparison, and verbal testimony are the pramanas.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading questions about the standard Nyaya pramana list.

passages.text: indriyārthasannikarṣotpannaṃ jñānam avyapadeśyam avyabhicāri vyavasāyātmakaṃ pratyakṣam
passages.translation_or_note: Perception is cognition produced by sense-object contact, non-verbal, non-deviating, and determinate.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading definitions of perception.

passages.text: atha tatpūrvakaṃ trividham anumānaṃ pūrvavac cheṣavat sāmānyato dṛṣṭaṃ ca
passages.translation_or_note: Inference, preceded by perception, is of three kinds: purvavat, sheshavat, and samanyato drshta.
Verbal testimony is the instruction of a trustworthy person.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading shabda and apta questions.

passages.text: Hetvabhasa is treated in Nyaya as a reason that appears to be probative but is defective in relation to the probandum, the subject, or the required pervasion.
passages.translation_or_note: This is a compact grader note, not a sutra quotation.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading conceptual explanations of inferential fallacy.
